_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
_meta.purpose: Comprehensive mathematical registry for the Higgins Unity Framework. Contains all formal definitions, theorems, lemmas, corollaries, proof sketches, canonical formulas, symbolic notation, numerical constants, fixed-point results, and open problems. This file appends to (does not replace) HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json and CLAIM_CLASSIFICATION.json. Both human scientists and AI systems should reference this as the complete mathematical foundation.
_meta.update_protocol: When a new theorem is proved or an open problem is resolved, add it here with date, evidence, and promotion status. This file is part of the refresh chain.

notation_table.purpose: Standard symbolic notation used throughout HUF. Where HUF engineering notation differs from standard CoDa literature, both forms are given.

S_D.definition: The D-part simplex: S^D = {x ∈ ℝ^D : x_i > 0, Σ x_i = 1}
S_D.note: The sample space for all compositional data in HUF

C.definition: Closure operator: C(x) = x / Σ x_i
C.note: Projects any positive vector onto the simplex

clr.definition: Centred log-ratio transform: clr(x)_i = ln(x_i) − (1/D) Σ_{j=1}^{D} ln(x_j)
properties: Maps simplex to hyperplane in ℝ^D
clr.note: The primary coordinate system for HUF analysis

ilr.definition: Isometric log-ratio transform: ilr(x) = Ψ^T · clr(x) where Ψ is an orthonormal basis (SBP matrix)
properties: Linear isometry from S^D to ℝ^{D−1}
Preserves Aitchison inner product as Euclidean inner product
ilr.note: Used for statistical analysis; SBP choice does not affect EITT

d_A.note: Measures compositional distance. Used for drift detection.

inner_product_A.definition: Aitchison inner product: ⟨x, y⟩_A = (1/D) Σ_{i<j} ln(x_i/x_j) · ln(y_i/y_j)

sigma_A_squared.definition: Aitchison variance (total variance): σ²_A = (1/D) Σ_{i=1}^{D} clr_i²
sigma_A_squared.note: The primary observable in PLL analysis. Its parabolic trajectory is the PLL lock signal.

properties: Minimum H → 0 as x approaches a vertex
H.note: The entropy whose near-invariance under decimation is the core EITT claim

D_M.definition: Block decimation operator: D_M maps a time series {x(t)}_{t=1}^{T} to {x̄_G^{(b)}}_{b=1}^{⌊T/M⌋} where x̄_G^{(b)} = C(∏_{t∈B_b} x(t)^{1/M})
D_M.note: Geometric-mean block averaging. The compression operator for EITT.

delta_M.note: Percentage entropy deviation. The primary EITT diagnostic.

N_eff.note: Number of 'effectively present' components. Ecological diversity equivalent.

perturbation_difference.note: Measures compositional change between consecutive time steps

theorem_1_vertex_theorem.name: Vertex Theorem (Orthogonality Condition)
theorem_1_vertex_theorem.statement: Let {x(t)} be a D-part compositional time series on S^D with differentiable CLR trajectories. Then σ²_A(t) = (1/D) Σ_{i=1}^{D} [clr_i(t)]². The vertex (extremum) of σ²_A occurs at t = t* where: d(σ²_A)/dt = (2/D) Σ_{i=1}^{D} clr_i(t) · clr_i'(t) = 0. This is equivalent to clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*) in the Aitchison inner product.

proof.step_4: By definition of the Aitchison inner product: ⟨clr(t*), clr'(t*)⟩ = 0, i.e., clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*). QED.

corollary_1.name: PLL Error Signal
corollary_1.statement: Near the vertex t₀, σ²_A(t) ≈ σ²_A(t₀) + a(t − t₀)² where a = (1/D) Σ [(clr_i')² + clr_i · clr_i''] evaluated at t₀. The error signal ε(t) = dσ²_A/dt = 2a(t − t₀) is linear near the lock point.
corollary_1.proof: Taylor expansion of σ²_A about t₀ to second order. The first-order term vanishes by T1. The coefficient of (t − t₀)² is the second derivative, which gives the 'ratio of 2' in the PLL gain.

corollary_2.name: Bowl vs Hill Classification
corollary_2.statement: If a > 0 (second derivative positive), σ²_A has a bowl (minimum) — the system is locked. If a < 0 (second derivative negative), σ²_A has a hill (maximum) — the system is relaxing (anti-lock).
examples: Hill (anti-lock): U-238 decay chain, Th-232 decay chain, stellar nucleosynthesis

corollary_3.name: Vertex Location as Transition Date
corollary_3.statement: When σ²_A(t) is parabolic with vertex at t₀, the year (or parameter value) t₀ marks the moment of dynamic equilibrium — where competing compositional forces are most balanced.
examples: California energy: vertex 2014 (solar/wind began to dominate)

theorem_2_clr_linearity.name: CLR Linearity Identity
theorem_2_clr_linearity.statement: For compositions x(1), ..., x(M) on S^D, the CLR of their geometric mean equals the arithmetic mean of their CLRs: clr(x̄_G) = (1/M) Σ_{t=1}^{M} clr(x(t)).

proof.step_1: x̄_G = C(∏ x(t)^{1/M}) by definition of the Aitchison barycenter.
proof.step_3: ln(x̄_G,i) = (1/M) Σ_t ln(x_i(t)) by properties of geometric mean.
proof.step_4: Substituting: clr(x̄_G)_i = (1/M) Σ_t [ln(x_i(t)) − (1/D) Σ_j ln(x_j(t))] = (1/M) Σ_t clr_i(x(t)). QED.

theorem_2_clr_linearity.significance: This identity is WHY geometric-mean decimation preserves CLR structure exactly. The arithmetic mean in CLR space IS the geometric mean on the simplex. This is the algebraic heart of EITT.

theorem_3_hessian_bound.name: EITT Hessian Bound (Second-Order)
theorem_3_hessian_bound.status: PROVED under stated assumptions (A1-A3). Sharp constants remain open.

assumptions.A1: The composition x(t) is a stationary ergodic process on S^D with finite second moments in CLR space.
assumptions.A2: The Aitchison variance σ²_A of the process satisfies σ²_A < δ · min(x̄_i) for some constant δ controlling the distance from the boundary.
assumptions.A3: The autocovariance function of the CLR process decays sufficiently fast that block means are asymptotically independent.

proof_sketch.step_1: CLR linearity (T2): clr(x̄_G^{(b)}) = (1/M) Σ clr(x(t)). Exact, no approximation.
proof_sketch.step_2: Shannon entropy H(x) = H(clr⁻¹(clr(x))). Taylor expand H around x̄ to second order.
proof_sketch.step_3: First-order term: 𝔼[Σ clr_i · ∂H/∂clr_i] = 0 by stationarity (deviations from mean have zero expectation).
proof_sketch.step_4: Second-order (Hessian) term: The Hessian of H on the simplex has eigenvalues bounded by −1/min(x_i). The trace contributes (D−1)·σ²_A/(2δ·M·H̄).
proof_sketch.step_5: Remainder: Under A3 (mixing), the O(M^{−3/2}) term is controlled by the mixing rate.

open_problems: O-1: Direct measurement of σ²_A and δ from raw data (currently back-computed)
O-2: Concentration inequality on |δ_M − 𝔼[δ_M]| using explicit mixing coefficients
O-3: Extension to slowly drifting (non-stationary) processes with drift velocity bound
O-4: Higher-dimensional sharpness — is the bound tight in D > 10?

lemma_1_balance_preservation.name: Balance Mean Preservation Under Decimation
lemma_1_balance_preservation.statement: Because D_M computes the arithmetic mean in CLR space, and ILR is a linear isometry from CLR, the ILR balance coordinates of the decimated series satisfy: ilr_k(x̄_G^{(b)}) = (1/M) Σ_{t∈B_b} ilr_k(x(t)).
lemma_1_balance_preservation.proof: Follows directly from T2 (CLR linearity) and the linearity of the ILR transform (Ψ is a constant orthonormal matrix). QED.

corollary.statement: The choice of SBP (sequential binary partition) does not affect EITT. Any ILR coordinate system gives the same balance-mean values under decimation.
corollary.significance: This is why EITT is robust to SBP choice — the linearity is exact in any orthonormal basis.

lemma_2_entropy_concavity.name: Shannon Entropy Concavity on the Simplex
lemma_2_entropy_concavity.statement: Shannon entropy H(x) = −Σ x_i ln(x_i) is strictly concave on S^D. The Hessian has eigenvalues: (∂²H/∂x_i²) = −1/x_i (diagonal), off-diagonal terms arise from the simplex constraint.
lemma_2_entropy_concavity.consequence_for_eitt: By Jensen's inequality applied to the concave function H: H(𝔼[X]) ≥ 𝔼[H(X)]. For block decimation: H(x̄_G) ≥ (1/M) Σ H(x(t)). This creates a systematic UPWARD bias in entropy under decimation. The EITT residual measures how small this bias actually is.
lemma_2_entropy_concavity.note: This is a known result from information theory. HUF's contribution is the empirical observation that the bias is extremely small (< 1% typically) for legitimate compositional time series.

result_1_adac_convergence.name: ADAC Iterative Convergence
result_1_adac_convergence.claim: The ADAC (Apportioned Diffraction Analysis Correction) mapping is contractive, converging to a fixed point by the Banach contraction mapping theorem.
result_1_adac_convergence.status: CLAIMED — numerical evidence supports, formal proof not in repo

banach_theorem_statement.formal: Let (X, d) be a complete metric space and T: X → X a contraction mapping with Lipschitz constant L < 1, i.e., d(Tx, Ty) ≤ L · d(x, y) for all x, y ∈ X. Then T has a unique fixed point x* ∈ X, and for any x₀ ∈ X, the iteration x_{n+1} = T(x_n) converges to x* with rate d(x_n, x*) ≤ L^n · d(x₀, x*) / (1 − L).

application_to_adac.space: (S^D, d_A) — the simplex with Aitchison metric (complete metric space)
application_to_adac.contraction_condition: |m| < 1 where m is the slope of the correction transfer function
application_to_adac.numerical_evidence: DADI error: 12.5% → 4.99% over 5 iterations. RMSE < 0.05 dB. Consistent with L ≈ 0.4.
application_to_adac.what_is_missing: Formal derivation of the contraction constant L from the physical parameters of the acoustic system. The mapping T is nonlinear and its Lipschitz constant depends on the geometry of the cabinet and the frequency dependence of diffraction.

result_1_adac_convergence.promotion_condition: Derive L analytically from cabinet geometry parameters. Show L < 1 for the class of physically realizable cabinets.

result_2_eitt_rg_fixed_point.name: EITT as Renormalization Group Fixed Point (Conceptual)
result_2_eitt_rg_fixed_point.claim: The EITT invariance condition dH/dM = 0 is structurally analogous to an RG fixed point. The entropy beta function β_H(M) = M · dH/dM = 0 iff the signal is at a 'legitimate' compositional process.
result_2_eitt_rg_fixed_point.status: ANALOGY — not a formal fixed-point result
result_2_eitt_rg_fixed_point.connection: In statistical mechanics, a renormalization group fixed point is a point where the system looks the same at all scales. EITT says: a legitimate compositional time series has the same entropy at all temporal resolutions. The structural analogy is: decimation by M is the 'blocking' step of real-space RG, and entropy invariance is the RG fixed-point condition.
result_2_eitt_rg_fixed_point.formal_status: This is a framing device, not a theorem. The analogy may inspire future formal results but does not currently constitute a proof of anything.
result_2_eitt_rg_fixed_point.important_distinction: Decimation is NOT a contraction mapping on (S^D, d_A). Aitchison distances EXPAND under decimation. EITT invariance is an entropy-level phenomenon, not a distance-level contraction.

result_3_h1_fixed_point.name: H1 Operator Fixed Point and Lyapunov Stability
result_3_h1_fixed_point.claim: The Higgins Operator H1|ψ⟩ = μ(|ψ⟩)·u has a unique fixed point at μ = 1 (full coherence). Lyapunov function V = 1 − μ proves global asymptotic stability: V̇ = −γ(1−μ)² ≤ 0, with V̇ = 0 iff μ = 1.
result_3_h1_fixed_point.status: CLAIMED — proof in external H1 paper (not in public repo). L0 classification.
result_3_h1_fixed_point.banach_extension: The contraction mapping theorem in the completed space guarantees existence and uniqueness of the fixed point.
result_3_h1_fixed_point.what_is_in_repo: Summary in science/quantum/HUF_VCore_Stack_Brief_v1.0.md (lines 167-179). The Lyapunov argument is sketched. Full proof steps are not reproduced.
result_3_h1_fixed_point.what_is_missing: The actual derivation of V̇ and the verification that the system is a contraction in the appropriate Banach space.

result_4_fixed_point_v3_2.name: HUF Refresh Chain Fixed Point v3.2
result_4_fixed_point_v3_2.status: ADMINISTRATIVE — not a mathematical fixed point
result_4_fixed_point_v3_2.meaning: The term 'FIXED POINT v3.2' in PHASE_MARKERS.json and FAST_REFRESH.json refers to a decision point in the project where the refresh chain was declared self-consistent and stable. It is an administrative marker, not a mathematical fixed point in the Banach or topological sense. The naming convention borrows from dynamical systems language to express: at this version, the system of documents is internally consistent and further changes should not break prior results.

canonical_values_and_constants.purpose: Complete registry of numerical constants that appear across HUF. Any document citing a different value should reference this table.

corner_frequency.note: L is the baffle dimension in metres

arithmetic_geometric_ratio.note: Factor of destruction by using arithmetic mean

lithium7_CS_ratio.note: Highest C/S ratio in any domain (cosmological lithium problem)

open_problems_registry.purpose: Every formally stated open problem in the HUF programme, with cross-references and promotion conditions.

O1_eitt_general_invariance.statement: Prove a general theorem: Under conditions C, |δ_M| < f(D, σ²_A, ρ₁, M) for all legitimate compositional time series.
O1_eitt_general_invariance.status: OPEN — the central open problem of the programme
O1_eitt_general_invariance.partial_progress: The Hessian Bound (T3) provides a second-order expected-value bound under assumptions A1-A3. What remains is: (a) a concentration inequality (tail bound), (b) verification of mixing conditions in real data, (c) sharp constants.
O1_eitt_general_invariance.if_solved: Would promote EITT from 'empirical invariance' to 'mathematical theorem'. The strongest possible result for the programme.

O2_pll_parabola_genericity.statement: Prove that σ²_A(t) is generically parabolic under mild regularity conditions (smooth, slowly-varying forces on the simplex).
O2_pll_parabola_genericity.partial_progress: The vertex theorem (T1) proves the orthogonality condition at the extremum. What remains is showing that σ²_A has exactly one extremum (parabolic shape) when forcing is slow relative to the system timescale.
O2_pll_parabola_genericity.if_solved: Would promote PLL universality from L1 to L2 (process control candidate).

O3_noise_conjugacy.statement: Prove that lock noise + anti-lock noise = 0 (observed empirically in all domains).
O3_noise_conjugacy.partial_progress: Observed in 10/10 domains. Physically plausible (polynomial residuals in complementary regimes should cancel). No formal proof.
O3_noise_conjugacy.if_solved: Would promote noise squeeze from L0 to L1.

O4_adac_contraction_constant.statement: Derive the Lipschitz constant L for the ADAC correction mapping from cabinet geometry parameters. Show L < 1 for physically realizable cabinets.
O4_adac_contraction_constant.partial_progress: Numerical evidence: L ≈ 0.4 from 5-iteration convergence. The contraction condition |m| < 1 is asserted.

O5_renyi_invariance.statement: Prove that geometric-mean decimation preserves the Rényi entropy family for q ∈ (0, ∞).
O5_renyi_invariance.partial_progress: Empirically confirmed for q ∈ [0.1, 5.0] across 3 domains.

O6_shape_functional_invariance.statement: Prove that any shape functional (function of ratios only, invariant to scaling) is EITT-invariant in the interior regime.
O6_shape_functional_invariance.status: OPEN — conceptual framework sketched in EITT_WHY_IT_WORKS.md §7

O7_tail_bound.statement: Derive a concentration inequality for |δ_M − 𝔼[δ_M]| using explicit mixing coefficients.

O8_nonstationary_extension.statement: Extend EITT bounds to slowly-drifting (non-stationary) processes with drift velocity as a parameter.

identity_registry.purpose: All named identities referenced across HUF, with their RWA-001 lineage.

peter_higgins.role: Framework author, principal researcher, sole operator
peter_higgins.employer: Brock Electronics (day job). HUF/RWA is the mission.

rogue_wave_audio.philosophy: Concepts are the product, not a loudspeaker. Ideas are the commodity.

huf.tagline: Compositional structure as an open-loop monitoring signal.

hivip.full_name: Higgins Iterative Validation and Integrity Protocol

btl.dual_meaning: Both the physical laboratory in Markham, Ontario and the loudspeaker system
btl.lineage: Named after Bell Telephone Labs (BTL) — developed stereo nearly a century ago

dadc_dadi_adac.full_name: Dimension-Apportioned Diffraction Correction / Diffraction-Apportioned Dimension Inference / Apportioned-Dimension Analysis Correction
dadc_dadi_adac.significance: The round-trip measurement chain from RWA engineering that became the universal DADC-DADI-ADAC chain in HUF

semf_formulas.purpose: The Semi-Empirical Mass Formula (SEMF) as used in HUF's nuclear programme. These formulas are not HUF inventions — they are standard nuclear physics written out for reference.

compositional_decomposition.description: HUF decomposes each nucleus into a 4-part composition on the simplex: x = C(E_V, E_S, E_C, E_A) where E_V = a_V·A, E_S = a_S·A^{2/3}, E_C = a_C·Z(Z−1)/A^{1/3}, E_A = a_A·(A−2Z)²/A. The closure operator C normalises to sum = 1.
compositional_decomposition.sigma2A_meaning: σ²_A of this 4-part composition measures how 'balanced' the four binding energy terms are. Light nuclei (shell effects, magic numbers) have high σ²_A (HOT). Actinides have low σ²_A (COLD) because the four terms scale smoothly.

changelog.action: Initial mathematical addendum created. Covers: notation table (14 symbols), 3 theorems with proofs, 2 lemmas, 3 corollaries, 4 fixed-point/Banach results, 8 open problems, complete canonical values registry, identity registry (per RWA-001), SEMF formulas.
changelog.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
changelog.note: This addendum APPENDS to existing documents. It does not replace HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json, CLAIM_CLASSIFICATION.json, or any discovery document.
